examples.title: Node Structure Definition in C
examples.description: Defines the fundamental node structure for a singly linked list
examples.explanation: The structure defines a self-referential structure where the 'next' pointer points to another node of the same type. malloc allocates memory dynamically, sizeof calculates memory needed, and we handle allocation failure.

examples.title: Insertion at Beginning in Python
examples.description: Implements inserting a new node at the beginning of the list
examples.explanation: The insert_at_beginning method creates a new node and makes its next pointer reference the current head, then updates head to point to the new node. This maintains the chain while adding at the front.

examples.title: Deletion Operations in C
examples.description: Implements deletion of first node and deletion after a given node
examples.explanation: DeleteFirst uses a temporary pointer to store the node being deleted, moves head to the next node, then frees the memory. DeleteAfter bypasses the target node by re-linking. Search traverses the list comparing each node's data.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for search, O(1) for deletions

examples.title: Singly Linked List Implementation in Java
examples.description: Complete implementation with insert, delete, and display operations
examples.explanation: Java implementation uses inner class Node. InsertAtEnd traverses to find the last node. FindMiddle uses the 'tortoise and hare' algorithm where slow pointer moves one step and fast moves two steps - when fast reaches end, slow is at middle.
